evals.prompt: I want to add SparkScan to my app. Here's my main activity: EmptyActivity.kt. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete SparkScan integration code into EmptyActivity.kt, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions com.scandit.datacapture:barcode as a Gradle dependency
Setup checklist mentions com.scandit.datacapture:core as a Gradle dependency
A concrete version number is used for the dependencies (not a [version] placeholder)
Setup checklist mentions the camera permission (CAMERA or AndroidManifest)
License key placeholder (e.g. YOUR_LICENSE_KEY or similar) is present
No symbologies except EAN13_UPCA and CODE128 are enabled
runOnUiThread is used inside the onBarcodeScanned callback

evals.prompt: Add SparkScan barcode scanning to ProductListActivity.kt. When a barcode is scanned, add it to the scannedProducts list and update the total label.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds SparkScan to ProductListActivity alongside the existing list view code. All existing fields, adapter setup, and helper methods are preserved.

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions com.scandit.datacapture:barcode as a Gradle dependency
Setup checklist mentions com.scandit.datacapture:core as a Gradle dependency
A concrete version number is used for the dependencies (not a [version] placeholder)
runOnUiThread is used inside the onBarcodeScanned callback
addProduct() method is preserved or its logic is incorporated

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my app, I want to reject it if the data is '123456789' and show an error message 'Wrong barcode' for 30 seconds before resuming scanning. All other barcodes should show success feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds SparkScanFeedbackDelegate conformance, implements getFeedbackForBarcode to return SparkScanBarcodeFeedback.Error for rejected barcodes and SparkScanBarcodeFeedback.Success for valid ones, and assigns feedbackDelegate on sparkScanView.

assertions.text: resumeCapturingDelay with 30 seconds is set on the error feedback
SparkScanBarcodeFeedback.Success() is returned for non-rejected barcodes
The rejection condition checks barcode.data == "123456789" or equivalent
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: My app is used in a warehouse where workers wear gloves. Enable hardware trigger button support so they can scan using physical device buttons.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables hardwareTriggerEnabled on SparkScanViewSettings and passes the settings to SparkScanView.

assertions.text: hardwareTriggerEnabled = true is set on SparkScanViewSettings
SparkScanViewSettings instance is created and configured before being passed to SparkScanView
SparkScanView.newInstance is called with the configured SparkScanViewSettings (not a fresh SparkScanViewSettings())
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to hide the standard SparkScan trigger button and instead use a custom button in my app's UI. When the custom button is tapped, scanning should start. When tapped again while scanning, it should pause.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets triggerButtonVisible = false, creates a custom button, and implements button logic that calls sparkScanView.startScanning() and sparkScanView.pauseScanning().

assertions.text: A custom button is created or referenced
sparkScanView.startScanning() is called in the button action
sparkScanView.pauseScanning() is called for pause functionality
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need a custom trigger button that changes text based on the scanner state. Show 'START SCANNING' in IDLE state and 'STOP SCANNING' in ACTIVE state.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements SparkScanViewUiListener, registers it with setListener, and updates the button text in onViewStateChanged based on IDLE/ACTIVE states.

assertions.text: Button text is set to 'START SCANNING' for non-active states
Button text is set to 'STOP SCANNING' for active state
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to set up SparkScan with an 'Aim-to-Scan' workflow for precise barcode scanning in crowded environments.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with defaultScanningMode set to SparkScanScanningModeTarget, then passes these settings to SparkScanView.

assertions.text: SparkScanScanningMode.Target( is used for the scanning mode
SparkScanScanningBehavior.SINGLE is passed to the scanning mode
SparkScanPreviewBehavior.DEFAULT is passed to the scanning mode
SparkScanView.newInstance is called with the configured SparkScanViewSettings
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, lifecycle) is preserved
